findings.summary: MT3608 boost converter output_rail incorrectly identified as the same net as input_rail; USB-C connector correctly flagged for missing VBUS ESD protection and VBUS decoupling

correct.description: The USB compliance check correctly identifies J11 (USB_C_Receptacle_USB2.0_16P) as having vbus_esd_protection=fail and vbus_decoupling=fail. The CC pulldown resistors (R20, R21 at 5.1k) are correctly detected as passing CC1 and CC2 pulldown checks. The TP5000 battery charger IC and MT3608 boost converter are correctly identified as switching regulators with inductors L2 and L1 respectively.

incorrect.description: The MT3608 (U2) boost converter shows both input_rail and output_rail as '__unnamed_27' (the VIN node). In this circuit, L1 connects VIN (__unnamed_27) to the SW node (__unnamed_28), which then feeds through Schottky diode D5 to the VOUT power rail. When tracing the inductor (L1) to find the output rail, the code finds L1's two nets are __unnamed_27 and __unnamed_28. Because SW is __unnamed_28, ind_n1 (__unnamed_27) != sw_net, so it assigns ind_n1 (__unnamed_27) as the output — the same as the input. The actual output VOUT goes through D5 and is a named power rail that is not traced. The boost converter topology requires following the diode past the SW node to find the true output rail.
